cluster_summaries.label: Pay & Benefits
cluster_summaries.summary: Employees generally value the competitive pay, retirement plans, and wellness programs provided by the organization.

cluster_summaries.label: Culture & Belonging
cluster_summaries.summary: Some staff report a collaborative and friendly environment, though this is not consistent across all clinical units.

cluster_summaries.label: Growth & Recognition
cluster_summaries.summary: Many employees appreciate opportunities for skill development, though some report limited growth paths in specific divisions.

cluster_summaries.label: Flexibility & Care
cluster_summaries.summary: A segment of the workforce reports positive work-life balance, particularly in nursing roles.

cluster_summaries.label: Safety & Fairness
cluster_summaries.summary: While some employees feel supported by the organization's values, others express concerns regarding trust and transparency.

cluster_summaries.label: Burnout & Workload
cluster_summaries.summary: Staff frequently cite understaffing and high patient volumes as primary drivers of exhaustion and dissatisfaction.

cluster_summaries.label: Unsafe or Unfair Culture
cluster_summaries.summary: Serious allegations of toxic behavior, including bullying and discrimination, highlight a critical area for management intervention.

cluster_summaries.label: Stagnation
cluster_summaries.summary: Employees report frustration with micromanagement and a lack of support for career advancement from leadership.

cluster_summaries.label: Disconnected Culture
cluster_summaries.summary: Reports of poor communication and lack of teamwork suggest isolation in certain departments.

cluster_summaries.label: Work-Life Pressure
cluster_summaries.summary: Inflexible scheduling and the pressure to use PTO during low-census periods negatively impact work-life balance for some.
